metadata.seed: I keep overthinking everything at work, and I'm scared I'm going to get fired even though nobody said anything.

patient.query: I keep overthinking everything at work, and I'm scared I'm going to get fired even though nobody said anything.

automatic_thoughts: I'm scared I'm going to get fired
I keep overthinking everything

entailment: Anxiety about saying the wrong thing (finding)

llm_response.response: I understand that you're feeling anxious about your job performance, and it seems like you're worrying a lot about potential mistakes. It's natural to feel this way sometimes, but let's try to find some ways to manage these feelings together. We can work on identifying situations that trigger these thoughts and explore healthier ways to approach them.

patient.query: Yeah, I think my job feels like this constant pressure cooker. I keep thinking if I mess up even a little, it’ll all come crashing down. It’s exhausting, really. I just want to feel good about what I'm doing, you know? But instead, I just keep worrying.

automatic_thoughts: if I mess up even a little, it’ll all come crashing down
I just want to feel good about what I'm doing
I just keep worrying

llm_response.response:
I can certainly relate to the feeling of being in a constant pressure cooker at work. It sounds like you're carrying a heavy burden of responsibility and fear of making mistakes. Let's take a moment to acknowledge that it's completely normal to feel this way sometimes, especially when we care deeply about our work.

However, I wonder if there might be other ways to approach your job that could help alleviate some of the pressure you're feeling? Together, we can explore some potential strategies and find a more balanced approach that works for you. Would you be open to discussing this further?

patient.query: I guess it makes sense that it’s normal to feel this way—but it doesn’t feel normal to me. I mean, every time I think about trying to approach things differently, I just get this wave of anxiety. What if I try something new and it doesn’t work? Then I’ll really be in trouble. It’s like I’m stuck in this loop, and I’m not sure how

triggers: thinking about trying to approach things differently
automatic_thoughts: what if I try something new and it doesn’t work?
I’ll really be in trouble

patient.query: Yeah, I guess it is distressing. I mean, I’ve always been the one who’s supposed to have it all together, so when I think about failing, it just feels... humiliating. I don't know, it’s like I’m afraid of what everyone will think of me. It’s hard to shake that feeling.

automatic_thoughts: I’ve always been the one who’s supposed to have it all together
when I think about failing, it just feels humiliating
I’m afraid of what everyone will think of me

neutral: Anxiety about saying the wrong thing (finding)

Would it be helpful to explore these thoughts together? We could examine evidence that supports and challenges them, and consider alternative interpretations that may help reduce your distress.

patient.query: I see what you mean, but it’s tough to believe that it’s okay to fail. I feel like if I mess up, people will see me as less capable. There’s this part of me that just thinks I should be doing better, like I have to prove myself all the time. I wish I could just let go of that pressure and not feel so trapped by it.

if I mess up, people will see me as less capable
I should be doing better
I have to prove myself all the time
